title: Emaré
summary: The repository artifact is a copy/version of University of Michigan CME item CME00009. That digital text manually represents the title page and the 1,035-line poem in Edith Rickert's 1907 Chicago dissertation, while omitting her preface, introduction, endnotes, glossary, index, colophon, and other book matter. Rickert transcribed the unique witness in British Library Cotton MS Caligula A II, ff. 71r-76v, with modern capitals and punctuation, marked expansions, additions, corrections, and apparatus consulting Ritson and Gough. Complete downloadable scans of Rickert, Gough, and Ritson's relevant volumes, plus White's later diplomatic transcription, are cached locally. No complete public digital object was found for the 210-folio physical codex: the only public manuscript photographs located cover the unrelated Siege of Jerusalem on ff. 111r-125r, omit Emaré, and are view-only under terms that prohibit downloading.

entities.label: General Corpus source XML for CME00009
entities.description: The XML artifact consumed by this repository's conversion and publication workflow, supplied through the Kyvero-Press/CME git submodule.
notes: IDG/@ID, BIBNO, and VID all identify the file as CME00009.
The CME submodule is a distinct repository; this manifest does not alter its source artifact.

entities.label: University of Michigan Corpus of Middle English Prose and Verse item CME00009
entities.description: A publicly accessible digital text representing Rickert's dissertation title page and edited poem, with structural markup for pages, stanzas, verse lines, manuscript leaves, marginal summaries, footnotes, and typographic expansions.

date_statements.label: repository XML structural review
date_statements.notes: The TEMPHEAD revision date documents review of this serialization, not necessarily the first online publication of the U-M item.

notes: U-M describes its 2016-2018 batch generically as manually keyed and proofread from public-domain editions; no item-specific production log was found.
TEXT/@LANG is 'eng', although the poem itself is Middle English; the coarse encoding attribute is not used as work-language authority.

entities.label: Rickert, The Romance of Emaré (Chicago dissertation, 1907)
entities.description: The immediate print source for CME00009: Rickert's revised dissertation edition of the unique manuscript text, with introduction, marginal prose summaries, textual footnotes, endnotes, glossary, and index.

bibliographic.citation: Edith Rickert, ed., The Romance of Emaré: Re-edited from the MS with Introduction, Notes, and Glossary (Chicago: Richard Clay & Sons, Limited, 1907).
bibliographic.title: The Romance of Emaré

notes: Rickert says the edition was prepared in 1898-1899, then completely revised before publication; this is edition history, not a date of the medieval work.

The text occupies printed pp. 1-32. CME00009 retains its marginal summaries and page-level footnotes but omits the preface, fifty-page introduction, endnotes on pp. 33-48, glossary, index, and colophon.
The separately cataloged EETS issue carries confusing 1906/1908 publication history and is not silently equated here with the exact 1907 Chicago title page encoded by CME00009.

entities.label: British Library, Cotton MS Caligula A II
alternate_labels: Cotton MS Caligula A II, ff. 3-139
former Cotton Vespasian D VIII and Vespasian D XXI
entities.description: A bound paper volume of two historically independent manuscript units. The first unit, ff. 3-139, is a Middle English miscellany containing the unique witness of Emaré on ff. 71r-76v; the second, ff. 142-210, contains Carthusian statutes and related records.

date_statements.label: first manuscript unit
date_statements.notes: DIME adopts the narrow dating also argued by Rickert from Lydgate's Nightingale and the chronicle additions.

date_statements.label: current British Library catalog date
date_statements.value: Second half of the fifteenth century

holding.notes: The exact component record for the Middle English miscellany is https://searcharchives.bl.uk/catalog/041-001102312.

physical_description.support: Paper; early-modern endleaves and inserted leaves; a parchment wrapper is reported for the second historical unit
physical_description.extent: 210 folios: early-modern endleaves ff. 1-2; first manuscript unit ff. 3-139; early-modern leaves ff. 140-141; second manuscript unit ff. 142-210
physical_description.dimensions: Bound volume approximately 225 x 180 mm; DIME reports the first unit's leaves as approximately 210 x 140 mm
notes: British Museum binding dated 1957.

The complete physical object contains covers, endleaves, blanks or inserted leaves, and many works adjacent to Emaré; these are not part of the intellectual work but belong in any complete-codex facsimile.
Emaré occupies ff. 71r-76v in the current foliation. Rickert and Gough record the legacy range as ff. 69-74.
The British Library parent and component records expose no current digitised-content link.
DIME reports only the Siege of Jerusalem folios as digitised; the public Siege archive offers images of ff. 111r-125r, not Emaré.

A complete reproduction request should ask for front and back covers or boards, spine if offered, pastedowns, flyleaves, blanks, inserted or detached material, and every recto and verso through f. 210, then map Emaré separately to ff. 71r-76v.

entities.label: Ritson, Ancient Engleish Metrical Romanceës (1802)
entities.description: The first printed edition of Emare, with the poem in volume 2 and original readings, notes, glossary, corrections, and conjectural emendations in volume 3; Rickert explicitly reports and compares it.

bibliographic.citation: Joseph Ritson, ed., Ancient Engleish Metrical Romanceës, 3 vols. (London: W. Bulmer and Company for G. and W. Nicol, 1802), 2:204-247; apparatus in 3:222, 323-333, 359-443.
bibliographic.title: Ancient Engleish Metrical Romanceës
bibliographic.publisher: W. Bulmer and Company for G. and W. Nicol

entities.label: Gough, Emare (1901)
entities.description: A normalized and substantially emended reconstruction of Emare. Rickert states that she obtained Gough late and used it chiefly in her notes.

bibliographic.citation: A. B. Gough, ed., Emare, Old and Middle English Texts 2 (London: Sampson Low Marston & Co.; New York: G. E. Stechert; Heidelberg: C. Winter, 1901).
bibliographic.title: Emare
bibliographic.publisher: Sampson Low Marston & Co.; G. E. Stechert; C. Winter

notes: Gough's text occupies pp. 1-34; it is not the direct text source of CME00009.

entities.label: White, BL Cotton Caligula Aii diplomatic transcription (2012)
entities.description: A later doctoral dissertation analyzing the first manuscript unit in context and providing a diplomatic transcription. Its Emaré transcription maps fols. 71r-76v across dissertation pp. 326-337.

bibliographic.citation: Denise C. White, 'BL Cotton Caligula Aii, Manuscript Context, The Theme of Obedience, and a Diplomatic Transcription Edition' (PhD diss., Georgia State University, 2012), doi:10.57709/3230402.
bibliographic.title: BL Cotton Caligula Aii, Manuscript Context, The Theme of Obedience, and a Diplomatic Transcription Edition

notes: White is a later comparison source and is not upstream of Rickert or the U-M encoding.
The downloadable repository PDF is a scholarly text and transcription, not a photographic facsimile of the manuscript.

entities.label: Internet Archive scan of Rickert's 1907 dissertation
entities.description: A complete 118-page PDF page-image scan retaining the dissertation title page, preliminary matter, full edition, blank leaves, colophon, and bound matter.
entities.reproduction_format: PDF page images with OCR text layer

entities.label: Internet Archive scan of Gough's 1901 edition
entities.description: A complete 64-page PDF page-image scan of Gough's book.
entities.reproduction_format: PDF page images with OCR text layer

entities.label: Internet Archive scan of Ritson 1802, volume 2
entities.description: A complete scan of the volume containing Ritson's Emare on pp. 204-247.
entities.reproduction_format: PDF page images with OCR text layer

entities.label: Internet Archive scan of Ritson 1802, volume 3
entities.description: A complete scan of the volume containing Ritson's readings, notes, glossary, corrections, and conjectural emendations for Emare.
entities.reproduction_format: PDF page images with OCR text layer

entities.label: Siege of Jerusalem Electronic Archive images of selected Cotton Caligula A II leaves
entities.description: Twenty-nine public color page views of ff. 111r-125r, the leaves carrying the Siege of Jerusalem. This selected-leaf surrogate omits Emaré on ff. 71r-76v and nearly all of the physical codex.
entities.reproduction_format: Zoomify color page images in a web viewer
notes: The provider permits free scholarly/private viewing but expressly prohibits downloading; no local copy was retained.
Coverage is partial relative to the physical manuscript and zero relative to the work-bearing folios.

agents.name: The Siege of Jerusalem Electronic Archive / Society for Early English and Norse Electronic Texts

subject.description: The complete local CME00009 XML artifact

object.description: University of Michigan CME item CME00009

scope.notes: Identifiers, title, structure, and text match; the exact transfer date and any intermediate normalization are undocumented.

object.description: Dissertation title page and edited poem

scope.notes: The encoding excludes Rickert's preface, introduction, endnotes, glossary, name index, colophon, blank leaves, and advertisements.

Rickert presents the text as she believed the scribe intended it to be read, with modern capitals and punctuation, marked expansions, additions, probable omissions, corrections, marginal summaries, and critical apparatus.

subject.description: Introduction, page footnotes, and endnotes reporting Ritson's readings and emendations

scope.notes: This is an apparatus-level comparison, not the base transcription source.

subject.description: Introduction, page footnotes, and endnotes reporting Gough's readings or proposed emendations

object.description: Gough's normalized edition and textual notes

scope.notes: Rickert says she obtained Gough late and used it chiefly in her notes; Gough is not her manuscript exemplar.

object.description: Emaré in the first manuscript unit

scope.notes: White also transcribes the surrounding first manuscript unit. This later edge is comparative and not upstream of CME00009.

object.description: Printed volume 2 containing Emare on pp. 204-247

subject.description: All twenty-nine public images in this selected-leaf web surrogate

scope.notes: The surrogate does not include Emaré on ff. 71r-76v, covers, endleaves, blanks, the second manuscript unit, or most adjacent works.

editorial_practices.description: Rickert expressly states that capitals are modern.

editorial_practices.representation: Modern editorial punctuation, with a few joined or separated words
editorial_practices.description: Rickert expressly states that punctuation is modern and that some word division was changed.

editorial_practices.source_feature: Scribal contractions, curls, strokes, and uncertain omissions
editorial_practices.representation: Expanded letters in italics; additions in square brackets; probable omissions in parentheses; selected ambiguous marks retained
editorial_practices.description: Rickert distinguishes expansions, additions, and probable omissions typographically while declining to reconstruct a normalized original text.
notes: The XML generally carries italic expansion letters as HI/@REND='italic' and retains bracketed or parenthesized editorial characters as text.

editorial_practices.source_feature: Ritson's 1802 text and apparatus and Gough's 1901 normalized reconstruction
editorial_practices.representation: R. and G. readings or emendations reported in page footnotes and endnotes
editorial_practices.description: Rickert compares both prior editions but makes the manuscript, not either printed predecessor, the transcription source.
CME00009 encodes the dissertation title page and printed pp. 1-32, including marginal summaries and page footnotes, but not Rickert's other preliminary or back matter.

access.access_method: Georgia State ScholarWorks item page and direct complete PDF

local_copies.label: Complete White dissertation and diplomatic transcription

work_portion.label: Emaré analysis and diplomatic transcription
notes: This complete scholarly dissertation contains a transcription, not manuscript photographs.
Repository PDF pages 337-348 visibly label manuscript columns from f. 71ra through f. 76vb.

access.access_method: British Library Archives and Manuscripts Catalogue plus reader registration and item request; consult current restrictions with Reference Services
notes: The parent record says a letter of introduction is required and that all manuscripts and archives must be consulted in London.

access.access_method: Date-bounded review of the current British Library parent and component records, BL digitised-manuscript discovery, DIME, Biblissima/Arlima discovery, and the Siege of Jerusalem Electronic Archive
access.format: No complete public digital object located
notes: The British Library records expose no digitised-content link.
DIME says only the Siege of Jerusalem folios have been digitised; those public images cover ff. 111r-125r and omit Emaré on ff. 71r-76v.

No public object covering the complete physical codex, covers, endleaves, blanks, adjacent works, and both codicological units was found. This is a dated negative search result, not proof that no private or institutional surrogate exists.

access.access_method: View-only project transcription with linked Zoomify manuscript images; provider terms prohibit downloading
notes: Coverage is partial for Cotton Caligula A II and excludes the whole CME00009 work portion, ff. 71r-76v.
No local copy is recorded because the exact terms state that images may not be downloaded.

access.access_method: British Library Digitisation Services online ordering, subject to preservation review, feasibility, quote, payment, and use terms
access.format: Digital reference or reproduction photography as approved
notes: A request for only 'Emaré' could produce a work excerpt rather than a complete-source facsimile. Request the entire physical codex and all offered exterior and endleaving surfaces, then identify ff. 71r-76v as the work portion.
The service states that original manuscripts are photographed rather than scanned and that reproduction permission is a separate question.

rights.basis: The text is a medieval anonymous work attested in a fifteenth-century manuscript; this assessment does not determine rights in editions, encodings, transcriptions, catalog text, or photographs.

rights.component: Rickert's 1907 edition, introduction, notes, and apparatus
rights.basis: The University of Michigan item presenting this edition-derived material expressly states that the materials are in the public domain and applies No Copyright-United States.
notes: No Copyright-United States is jurisdiction-specific and is not a worldwide license or dedication.

rights.component: Materials presented in U-M CME item CME00009
rights.basis: The current item page states that the materials are in the public domain and applies No Copyright-United States.
notes: The statement does not automatically license third-party interface assets or establish a worldwide status.

rights.component: The local XML serialization and repository-specific changes
rights.jurisdiction: Repository terms and intended place of reuse
rights.basis: U-M applies No Copyright-United States to the upstream item, but the local XML supplies no express license governing this serialization or later repository changes.

rights.component: Internet Archive page images, OCR layer, and PDF packaging
rights.jurisdiction: Provider terms and intended place of reuse

rights.component: White's dissertation text and repository PDF
rights.basis: The dissertation bears a 2012 copyright notice in Denise Carol White's name; the public ScholarWorks download did not expose an open reuse license.
notes: Open repository access supports consultation and local research caching, not unrestricted republication.

rights.component: The twenty-nine British Library manuscript images exposed through the Siege of Jerusalem Electronic Archive
rights.basis: The image viewers state Copyright British Library Board, all rights reserved. The project terms limit images to scholarly or private viewing, require written permission for other uses, and expressly prohibit downloading.
notes: The images were not cached after the restriction was verified.

evidence.citation: General Corpus / CME source XML, CME00009.xml.
evidence.summary: Identifies CME00009 in IDG/@ID, BIBNO, and VID; reproduces Rickert's dissertation title page and complete poem; carries 86 stanza groups, 1,035 verse-line elements, printed-page milestones, manuscript-leaf milestones, marginal summaries, footnotes, italic expansions, and a 2016 review note.

Counts 86 LG, 1,035 L, 32 body PB, 345 line-number MILESTONE, 12 leaf MILESTONE, 105 footnotes, 283 marginal notes, 1,147 HI, 36 ABBR, and one SUP element. Leaf milestones run from 71 through 76 verso.

evidence.citation: University of Michigan Library, Corpus of Middle English Prose and Verse, item CME00009.
evidence.locator: item information, publication, rights, contents, and full text
evidence.summary: Identifies the title, Rickert, Chicago publication by R. Clay & Sons in 1907, the title-page and Emare content units, public access, and the No Copyright-United States assertion.

evidence.citation: University of Michigan Library, 'About the Corpus of Middle English Prose and Verse.'
evidence.locator: collection history, 2016-2018 additions, production, markup, and bulk XML availability
evidence.summary: States that the later CME batches were manually keyed from public-domain editions, proofread and edited in house, and marked up with basic TEI semantics. It is collection-level context, not an item-specific production log.

evidence.citation: Edith Rickert, ed., The Romance of Emaré: Re-edited from the MS with Introduction, Notes, and Glossary (Chicago: Richard Clay & Sons, Limited, 1907).
evidence.locator: title page; preface; contents; introduction pp. ix-l; text pp. 1-32; notes pp. 33-48; glossarial index pp. 49-54; index p. 55; colophon
evidence.summary: The immediate edition names Rickert and the Chicago dissertation, identifies Cotton Caligula A II and Emaré on ff. 71-76, dates and describes the manuscript, prints the complete 1,035-line poem, and separates its introduction, poem, notes, glossary, and index.

evidence.citation: Rickert, The Romance of Emaré (1907), introduction section 2, 'Editions.'
evidence.summary: Rickert characterizes Ritson's and Gough's editions, states her aim to give the text as the scribe intended it to be read, declares modern capitalization and punctuation, defines italic expansions, bracketed additions, and parenthesized probable omissions, and explains which scribal signs she retained.

evidence.citation: British Library Archives and Manuscripts Catalogue, Cotton MS Caligula A II, record 040-001102311.
evidence.locator: parent record, hierarchy, access, physical characteristics, and blank Digitised Content field
evidence.summary: Confirms the current shelfmark, two-item hierarchy, 210-folio paper codex, early modern leaves, dimensions, 1957 binding, London consultation route, and absence of a current digitised-content link.

evidence.citation: British Library Archives and Manuscripts Catalogue, Cotton MS Caligula A II, ff. 3-139, record 041-001102312.
evidence.locator: component title/contents, date, origin, custodial history, access, and blank Digitised Content field
evidence.summary: The exact institutional component record identifies Emare as Boffey 1766 on ff. 71r-76v, dates the unit to the second half of the fifteenth century, assigns origin to England, lists its surrounding contents, and exposes no digitised-content link.

evidence.locator: date, place, form, keywords, plot summary, manuscript, and editions
evidence.summary: Dates composition to the late fourteenth century, leaves composition place uncertain between the Northeast Midlands and East Anglia, describes the twelve-line tail-rhyme stanza, assigns IMEV 1766, identifies the unique witness on ff. 71r-76v, and supplies the plot and edition history.

evidence.citation: Database of Middle English Romance, 'London, British Library, MS Cotton Caligula A.ii.'
evidence.locator: date, place, material, dimensions, codicological note, digitization note, and complete first-unit contents
evidence.summary: Dates the first unit 1446-1460, localizes it to the South-East or South-East Midlands, describes paper and dimensions, explains the two formerly independent manuscripts, maps Emaré to ff. 71r-76v and 1,035 lines, and states that only the Siege of Jerusalem folios have been digitised.

evidence.citation: A. B. Gough, ed., Emare, Old and Middle English Texts 2 (London: Sampson Low, Marston & Co.; New York: G. E. Stechert; Heidelberg: C. Winter, 1901).
evidence.locator: title page; table of contents; preface pp. vii-xi; text pp. 1-34; notes and glossary pp. 35-38
evidence.summary: Identifies the unique witness, maps new ff. 71-76 to old ff. 69-74, describes Gough's reconstruction and normalization policy, prints the poem, and documents Ritson as the prior edition.

evidence.citation: Joseph Ritson, ed., Ancient Engleish Metrical Romanceës, vol. 2 (London: W. Bulmer and Company for G. and W. Nicol, 1802).
evidence.summary: Contains the first printed text of Emare, the prior edition explicitly reviewed and cited by Rickert.

evidence.citation: Joseph Ritson, ed., Ancient Engleish Metrical Romanceës, vol. 3 (London: W. Bulmer and Company for G. and W. Nicol, 1802).
evidence.locator: Emare original readings p. 222; notes pp. 323-333; glossary pp. 359-435; corrections p. 440; conjectural emendations p. 443
evidence.summary: Contains the dispersed Emare apparatus that Rickert identifies in her edition history.

evidence.citation: Denise C. White, 'BL Cotton Caligula Aii, Manuscript Context, The Theme of Obedience, and a Diplomatic Transcription Edition' (PhD diss., Georgia State University, 2012), doi:10.57709/3230402.
evidence.locator: manuscript context pp. 1-26; Emaré analysis pp. 123-155; diplomatic transcription pp. 326-337
evidence.summary: Provides a later codex-context study and diplomatic transcription. Its repository PDF maps Emaré continuously from f. 71ra through f. 76vb and bears a 2012 copyright notice.

evidence.citation: The Siege of Jerusalem Electronic Archive, British Library MS Cotton Caligula A.ii (C), diplomatic transcription and linked manuscript images.
evidence.summary: Exposes color manuscript images for the Siege of Jerusalem on ff. 111r-125r. The selection contains no images of Emaré on ff. 71r-76v and is not a whole-codex surrogate.

evidence.citation: The Siege of Jerusalem Electronic Archive, Terms and Conditions.
evidence.locator: image rights, permitted viewing, permission requirements, and download restriction
evidence.summary: States that manuscript-image rights remain with holding libraries, permits unpaid scholarly or private viewing, requires written permission for other uses, and says images may not be downloaded.

evidence.locator: online ordering, required shelfmark and folio information, manuscript photography, preservation limits, delivery, and reproduction permissions
evidence.summary: Provides a priced route to request collection images, distinguishes reference from reproduction photography, says original manuscripts are photographed rather than scanned, reserves preservation review, and keeps reuse permission separate.

open_questions.question: Can a complete, publicly usable photographic object of British Library Cotton MS Caligula A II be obtained?
next_steps: Ask British Library Digitisation Services whether a complete existing surrogate or new complete reference-photography order is feasible for the 210-folio bound codex.

Specify front and back covers or boards, spine if offered, pastedowns, flyleaves, blanks, every recto and verso, inserted or detached material, and both codicological units; identify Emaré ff. 71r-76v only as the work mapping.
Keep any supplied image-use terms and publication permission separate from research access and from the medieval text's status.

open_questions.question: What exact production history created U-M item CME00009 and the XML now in the CME submodule?
next_steps: Seek item-level U-M production metadata or a raw distribution package that dates the first keying and documents correction passes.
Treat the 2016 TEMPHEAD entry as a review date and the collection-level manual-keying statement as context until item-specific evidence is found.

review_notes: The complete cached Rickert, Gough, Ritson volume 2, Ritson volume 3, and White PDFs were opened and checked for identity, pagination, work boundaries, and editorial statements.

A provisional bundle of the selected Siege of Jerusalem images was deleted immediately after the exact provider terms prohibiting download were verified; no restricted image bytes remain in source-cache/CME00009.
The whole-manuscript negative finding is dated and scoped. It does not assert that the British Library lacks internal photography, microfilm, or an orderable surrogate.
